title: Kimi CLI brings Moonshot’s coding agent into the terminal
friendlyTitle: Kimi CLI is an open coding helper for the terminal
summary: Kimi CLI is Moonshot AI’s open-source terminal coding agent, with roughly 9,800 GitHub stars and a recent 1.49.0 release. It supports shell and file workflows, reusable agent skills, multiple deployment paths, and ongoing work across Python and TypeScript packages.
friendlySummary: Kimi CLI lets an AI read files, use terminal tools, and help with code. The project is open source and has almost 10,000 GitHub stars.
whyItMatters: Developers can use it as a scriptable coding assistant inside an existing terminal workflow and inspect or extend the agent rather than relying on a closed desktop client. It is also a useful reference implementation for teams comparing coding-agent architecture, tool permissions, and skill systems.
friendlyWhyItMatters: You can use it for coding jobs or study the code to learn how terminal AI agents are built.
howItsUseful: Developers can use it as a scriptable coding assistant inside an existing terminal workflow and inspect or extend the agent rather than relying on a closed desktop client. It is also a useful reference implementation for teams comparing coding-agent architecture, tool permissions, and skill systems.
